one_time_description: Legal settlements ($66,354), interest on long-term debt ($563,774 expensed as other item), unrecoverable GST ($23,325) - multiple non-recurring other expense items totaling $1,372,745 net
economic_notes: First Nation receives approximately 67% of annual revenue from Settlement Trust and 18% from federal government transfer payments. Settlement Trust balance at March 31, 2020 was $72,647,086. Salt River First Nation Development Corporation is a wholly-owned business entity accounted for by modified equity method.
notes: Nation located in Northwest Territories (NT), not a province. Fiscal year end March 31, 2020. Salt River First Nation Settlement Trust ($72.6M) is excluded from consolidated statements as it is administered on behalf of third parties. Salt River First Nation Development Corporation consolidated using modified equity method (GBE). Nation is non-compliant with First Nations Financial Transparency Act (Note 13) - did not submit/post financial statements within 120 days. Financial Management System Certificate not yet provided to First Nations Finance Authority as of March 31, 2020. Long-term debt secured by tobacco/fuel rebates and resource revenues. Prior year revenue was significantly higher ($7.1M vs $4.5M) largely due to higher Settlement Trust distributions ($4.5M vs $3.0M).
